# exasol_user (Resource)

Creates, updates (rename / change auth) and drops an Exasol user.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# Password authentication
resource "exasol_user" "analyst" {
  name      = "ANALYST_USER"
  auth_type = "PASSWORD"
  password  = var.analyst_password
}

# LDAP authentication
resource "exasol_user" "ldap_user" {
  name      = "LDAP_USER"
  auth_type = "LDAP"
  ldap_dn   = "cn=ldap_user,dc=example,dc=com"
}

# OpenID authentication
resource "exasol_user" "openid_user" {
  name           = "OPENID_USER"
  auth_type      = "OPENID"
  openid_subject = "user@example.com"
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `auth_type` (String) Authentication type: "PASSWORD", "LDAP" or "OPENID".
- `name` (String) User name. Exasol user names are case-insensitive.

### Optional

- `ldap_dn` (String) LDAP distinguished name if auth_type is LDAP.
- `openid_subject` (String) OpenID subject if auth_type is OPENID.
- `password` (String, Sensitive) Password for PASSWORD authentication.

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) Terraform ID — always set to the user name in uppercase.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
terraform import exasol_user.analyst ANALYST_USER
```
